LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY'S MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS CONTINUED TO JULY 31, 2019. OTIS SEEKS TO HAVE THE COURT CONFIRM THAT THE SETTLEMENT IS IN GOOD FAITH PER TECH-BILT, INC. V. WOODWARD-CLYDE & ASSOCIATES (1985) 38 CAL.3D 488, BUT HAS NOT PROVIDED A COPY OF THE SETTLEMENT TO THE COURT NOR DISCLOSED ALL OF ITS MATERIAL TERMS. OTIS IS ORDERED TO FILE A MOTION TO SEAL IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 2.550 ET SEQ. BY JULY 19, 2019 AND PERSONALLY OR E-MAIL SERVE THAT MOTION BY THAT DATE ON ALL PARTIES. THE MOTION TO SEAL SHALL INCLUDE A COPY OF THE SETTLEMENT AND EXPLAIN WHY THERE IS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST IN SEALING IT OR PORTIONS THEREOF. (SEE CRC 2.550(D).) THE MOTION TO SEAL WILL BE HEARD ON JULY 31, 2019. OPPOSITION TO THAT MOTION, IF ANY, MAY BE FILED AND PERSONALLY OR E-MAIL SERVED BY JULY 25, 2019. ALL PARTIES TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR FILINGS PER SF LOCAL RULE 2.7B. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, NOT REPORTED.

MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR MAY-11-2020 CONTINUED TO MASTER CALENDAR JURY ON JUL-13-2020 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206 DUE TO COVID-19. NO APPEARANCES FOR PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT AT TRIAL CALL. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: D. LOK; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206) [AFTER HEARING ON 5/18/2020 - THE COURT GRANTED ATTY. JOSEPH R. ZOUCHA'S ORAL REQUEST TO CONTINUE THE JULY 13, 2020 TRIAL DATE TO NOVEMBER 16, 2020. 9:30 AM, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, KATHRYN T. CAMERLENGO APPEARED THROUGH COURT CALL AND WAS INFORMED THAT APPEARANCE IS ON THE WRONG TRIAL DATE, AND WAS FURTHER INFORMED THAT THE 5/11/20 TRIAL DATE WAS CONTINUED TO 7/13/20. ATTY. CAMERLENGO WAS THEN RELEASED FROM COURT COURT. NO OTHER APPEARANCES. 10:15 AM, AT THE END OF THE 5/18/2020 TRIAL CALL, DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL, JOSEPH ZOUCHA APPEARED THROUGH COURT CALL AND REQUESTED THE COURT TO CONTINUE THE 7/13/20 TO 11/16/20 PER AGREEMENT OF PARTIES. ATTY. ZOUCHA REPRESENTED TO THE COURT THAT HE HAD PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL ON HIS CELL PHONE AND BOTH CONFIRMED THAT PARTIES AGREED TO THE 11/16/2020 TRIAL DATE. THE COURT GRANTED THE REQUEST TO CONTINUE THE 7/13/20 TRIAL DATE TO 11/16/20 AND ORDERED DEFENDANT TO GIVE NOTICE OF THE NEW TRIAL DATE. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: D. LOK; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)]

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. JOSEPH R. ZOUCHA, ESQ. (Tel: 650-261-9600) appearing for plaintiff Quan Do;
Atty. KYLE CLAWSON, ESQ. of Dentons US LLP (Tel: 415-882-5000) appearing for defendant Otis Elevator Company.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

Matter submitted without argument. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Otis Elevator Company's motion for determination of good faith settlement is granted. When determining whether a settlement was entered into in good faith the court takes into account a rough approximation of plaintiff's total recovery and defendant's proportionate liability, the amount paid in settlement, and recognizes that a settlor should pay less in settlement than if he were found liable after trial. The court also considers the financial condition of the settlor, the existence of insurance, and any evidence of collusion. (See Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) In this case, the parties have not approximated plaintiff's total recovery and his statement of damages indicates that his damages are unsettled. (Chiu Decl., Ex. L.) Nevertheless, substantial evidence supports a finding that the settlement is in good faith. (See Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1350, n.6.) Otis presents evidence that its elevator was properly maintained and free from defect. (Chiu Dec., Ex. H [Hollinger Decl.] Since this evidence indicates that Otis has little if any potential liability, the settlement is in good faith regardless of the amount of plaintiff's claimed damages. (See Cahill v. San Diego Gas & Electric Co. (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 939, 962-966 [settlement amount of $25,000 was within ballpark of good faith settlements under Code of Civil Procedure section 877.6, despite plaintiff's multi-million dollar damages claim, when plaintiff had little if any chance of recovery at trial].) The objecting party (Garber) fails to meet its burden of showing, based on the information available at the time of settlement, that the settlement lacks good faith. (See Code Civ. Proc., 877.6(d).).

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185, E-MAIL: tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Settlement Agreement Under Seal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. JOSEPH R. ZOUCHA, ESQ. (Tel: 650-261-9600) appearing for plaintiff Quan Do;
Atty. KYLE CLAWSON, ESQ. of Dentons US LLP (Tel: 415-882-5000) appearing for defendant Otis Elevator Company.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY'S Motion To File Settlement Agreement Under Seal.

Matter submitted without argument. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Otis Elevator Company's motion to seal is denied. Assuming there is an overriding interest in enforcing the parties' contract and encouraging settlement, Otis has not shown a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed. Otis's unsupported speculation that the disclosure of the settlement could mislead the public into believing that it is admitting liability or could result in disparagement of Otis's name is insufficient to meet its burden, particularly given the modest amount of the settlement. (See Huffy Corp. v. Superior Court (2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 97, 105-107 [reversing trial court's order sealing documents including settlement agreement which had a confidentiality provision; "before papers can be sealed, there must be substantial prejudice to an overriding interest if the documents are made available to the public"]. So far as the record discloses, "[n]o prejudice to defendant's legitimate business and propriet[ar]y interests will occur if the settlement agreement is ordered unsealed." (Id. at 107.).

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185, E-MAIL: tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-15-2019 Continued To Jul-31-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

Continued to July 31, 2019. Otis seeks to have the court confirm that the settlement is in good faith per Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, but has not provided a copy of the settlement to the court nor disclosed all of its material terms. Otis is ordered to file a motion to seal in compliance with CRC 2.550 et seq. by July 19, 2019 and personally or e-mail serve that motion by that date on all parties. The motion to seal shall include a copy of the settlement and explain why there is an overriding interest in sealing it or portions thereof. (See CRC 2.550(d).) The motion to seal will be heard on July 31, 2019. Opposition to that motion, if any, may be filed and personally or e-mail served by July 25, 2019. All parties to provide courtesy copies of their filings per SF Local Rule 2.7B.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPU)
